text:
Once upon an autumn evening, in the sleepy town of Everwood, there lay a quiet park. Girls slid rails downstairs endless parks lay endless. Cats snoozed under velvet leaves. Old willows tossed silk-like droves.

Our heroine, Libby, loved just that kind of thing. She'd sneak into an antique shop one twilight just to glean unusual finds. That night, she picked out a peculiar orb. It glowed softly.

Moonlit. Only moonlight. It called to her.

Underneath midnight stars, she'd fly. But where?

How about ancient forests? Trees that whispered secrets.

So she'd weave there. And find old things.

Then one thing led to another. She knew secrets of old castles.

Castle halls familiar.

Late night whispers secrets.

Late night tales.

Her owl feather.

The moon guided her. Stories chased.

Then home.

She found.

Just a book.

Stars. That night.

Finds tales.

But here.

Weave. Stories
